channel.title: network on Geoff Dixon > Write-Verbose
channel.description: Recent content in network on Geoff Dixon > Write-Verbose

item.title: DIY Wireless Suppression with WMI and PowerShell
item.description: When you drop a laptop into a docking station with an Ethernet cable, you expect it to use the wired connection instead of than WIFI. This may not be the case on Windows unless wireless is supressed. This post will cover the problem, common 3rd-party solutions and a built-in solution using WMI and PowerShell Wireless Suppression prevents a WIFI connection being used when Ethernet is available. Why is this a problem?
